reason_not_drift: Every rejected construction or camera pass was stopped by its required pre-save, paired, or internal-visibility gate before presentation as an approved result.

attempts.cause: Treatment-strip edges terminated on adjoining perimeter faces and the inherited sidewall order faced inward.
attempts.disposition: No Blender source was saved. The empty attempt root and rejection marker are quarantined locally.

attempts.cause: The inset grid closed the perimeter, but diagonal source-pixel contacts and inward sidewall order remained.
Face winding was corrected, but Blender treated the extremely thin fan triangles around the treatment grid as zero-area.
All diagonal point contacts were split without adding or removing source pixels; the remaining failure was only Blender's thin-triangle area tolerance.

attempts.artifact_status: quarantined; each saved file passed individually, paired audit failed
attempts.cause: Rune and metal treatment grids had different internal breakpoints, so their topology differed despite identical outer bounds.
attempts.disposition: Both saved attempts and their local manifests are quarantined in distinct local output roots and have no downstream authority.

attempts.artifact_status: invalid; rejected internal staging; never published
attempts.cause: The direct end-face and matched topology camera trimmed the lower head transition too tightly.
attempts.disposition: Preserved only in temporary staging with an internal rejection marker; replaced by the wider matched camera pass.
